title: Murder Beneath the Clocktower Snow
setting: Blackstone Hall, a snowbound estate converted into a private criminology retreat

victim.name: Professor Adrian Wren
victim.description: A celebrated criminologist who planned to expose fraud at the retreat.

culprit.name: Julian Pike
culprit.description: The estate's head of security and the victim's former research subject.
culprit.relationship_to_victim: past subject with a hidden grudge
culprit.means: Controlled camera outages and had access to the antique display weapons.
culprit.motive: The victim was about to reveal Julian had fabricated data in a famous cold case study.
culprit.opportunity: Used a staged security sweep to move unseen through the east stair.
culprit.alibi: Claims he was resetting a generator fault in the basement at 9:12 PM.

suspects.name: Evelyn Cross
suspects.description: The retreat director whose finances depend on the professor's endorsement.
suspects.means: Access to the estate's service corridors and maintenance keys.
suspects.motive: Feared exposure of embezzlement tied to retreat funds.
suspects.opportunity: Met the victim shortly before the lights failed.
suspects.alibi: Claims she was greeting donors in the west salon at 9:05 PM.

suspects.name: Miles Dorian
suspects.description: A bestselling true-crime author accused by the victim of plagiarism.
suspects.means: Handled rare toxins during research for a previous book.
suspects.motive: Wanted to stop the victim from ruining his reputation at the keynote.
suspects.opportunity: Was seen near the library corridor ten minutes before the death.
suspects.alibi: Says he was recording notes alone in the observatory at 9:10 PM.

suspects.name: Nora Bell
suspects.description: The victim's graduate assistant who depended on him for recommendation letters.
suspects.means: Organized the victim's tea service and handled his schedule.
suspects.motive: Learned that the victim intended to sabotage her fellowship application.
suspects.opportunity: Delivered the victim's tea before the lecture.
suspects.alibi: States she stayed in the archive room cataloging papers at 9:00 PM.

suspects.name: Julian Pike
suspects.description: The estate's head of security and the victim's former research subject.
suspects.relationship_to_victim: past subject with a hidden grudge
suspects.means: Controlled camera outages and had access to the antique display weapons.
suspects.motive: The victim was about to reveal Julian had fabricated data in a famous cold case study.
suspects.opportunity: Used a staged security sweep to move unseen through the east stair.
suspects.alibi: Claims he was resetting a generator fault in the basement at 9:12 PM.

motive: The victim was about to reveal Julian had fabricated data in a famous cold case study.
method: Julian disabled the east corridor camera, lured the victim to the map room, then used a puncture device coated with fast-acting digitalis to mimic a sudden collapse.

true_timeline.summary: Guests arrive for the retreat gala while snow closes the access road.
The victim privately tells Nora that a public accusation will be made tonight.
Julian initiates a fake maintenance alert and quietly disables the east corridor camera.
Nora delivers tea to the victim, unaware Julian has already tampered with the route.
Julian messages the victim about an urgent discovery in the map room.
The victim is killed in the map room with a poisoned puncture wound.
Julian triggers a brief power fluctuation to scramble witness recollection and coverage.
The body is discovered and the estate is locked down.

evidence_items.name: Cracked teacup
evidence_items.description: A teacup bearing Nora's fingerprints but no poison residue.

evidence_items.name: Deleted security segment
evidence_items.description: A seven-minute gap in east corridor footage manually triggered from the security desk.

evidence_items.name: Digitalis trace
evidence_items.description: A toxicology trace on the victim's collar consistent with digitalis delivery by puncture.

evidence_items.name: Missing display spike
evidence_items.description: A display label from an antique navigation spike case now missing one item.

evidence_items.name: Burned ledger scrap
evidence_items.description: A scrap from the retreat accounts suggesting Evelyn hid expenses.

evidence_items.name: Unsigned apology draft
evidence_items.description: A draft apology in Miles's notebook after the victim threatened exposure.

evidence_items.name: Basement boot print
evidence_items.description: Wet tread marks matching Julian's boots lead from the control panel toward the east stair.

evidence_items.name: Phone message extract
evidence_items.description: A recovered message summoning the victim to the map room from Julian's device.

evidence_items.name: Cut generator wire
evidence_items.description: A deliberately nicked wire that caused the brief outage used to support Julian's alibi.

red_herrings.explanation: Nora appears suspicious because she served the victim's tea, but the poison was not ingested.
Evelyn hid financial fraud, giving her motive to lie, but that misconduct is separate from the murder.

notes: Every polished alibi cracked once the evidence was forced into sequence.
